visuals.title: Lecture Note: Second-Order System Setup
visuals.caption: The note introduces the second-order form, its poles, and the corresponding magnitude and phase expressions.

images.caption: For zeta greater than or equal to one, the response is interpreted as a low-pass filter.
The note compares the one-pole and two-pole low-pass curves and begins the complex-pole case.

visuals.title: One Pole Versus Two Poles
visuals.description: The two-pole response falls more sharply than the one-pole response for the example in the note.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Complex Poles
visuals.caption: The note rewrites the denominator in terms of zeta omega_n and omega_d to explain the underdamped case.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Resonant Example
visuals.caption: For zeta=0.1 and omega_n=10, the sketched magnitude shows a pronounced peak near the damped frequency.
